generatedSentence: Elias, a lonely, unkempt techie, lived in a secluded cabin during the harsh winter, subsisting on blueberry muffins and stale donuts with strong arabica coffee. His days were spent staring at an old browser on his screen, a calculator nearby, obsessing over the monetary value of an ancient artifact he’d found. He often drifted into a pleasant fantasy of a better life, a stark contrast to his current unhappiness. A faded photograph of an obelisk on his wall served as a cryptic reminder of an old issue. He used to be a muscle-bound security expert, a life he left behind, now just lifting weights made from scrap metal covered in wool. He felt naturally drawn to investigate the indirect clues he'd gathered about the artifact's true origin, a way to broaden his purpose. The tranquility was shattered when news broke: the notorious kingpin, a cutthroat mastermind known for betrayal, had resurfaced, seeking vengeance. The criminal underworld was abuzz. Elias knew this meant his past was catching up. The unsettling noise of high-powered engines outside, at the junction near the old gate by the railroad tracks, signaled trouble. He saw a frigate of black SUVs, their engines revving with petrol and ignition engaged, moving forwards. It was the cardinal sign of an attack, led by an iconic enforcer. Elias felt his heart utterly twisted as he realized his old limiter of self-imposed isolation was now kaput. He had been kicked back into the game, hearing their distant heckle and mocking laughter. Elias, driven by a deep sense of loyalty to an old partner and the need to rescue something precious, made a stand. He fortified his safe house, a former hangar near an old embassy, now adorned with a single gladiolus in a miniature vase. The attackers were jackhammering at the reinforced brick walls. Elias, with the magic of his improvised tech, turned the tide. He launched explosive coconut shells and strategically placed firestone charges, creating an enormous diversion. He needed to outlast them, fueled by memories of shared honour. He knew the keystone to their plans was an ancient map he possessed, which he protected like an apple. He saw their models falter, their resolve beginning to bloom. As the dust settled, Elias surveyed the darkened, debris-strewn hangar. He had won, but not inexpensively. The iconic kingpin was gone, leaving only vermin behind. His victory felt hopeful, and the silence, once a burden, now held a new philosophy. He brewed some cookery, a simple bacon and pickle dish, contemplating his next move. He was no longer just a viewer of his own life; he was its gamekeeper. The novelty of retirement had worn off. He felt a quiet satisfaction, knowing he had protected something round and blunt – the truth. He swore to hunt down the remaining operatives, inching closer to a final peace. His life, utterly changed, was now featuring a new path. He’d been ticked off the list of the forgotten, no longer a mere jongleur in the shadows.
panelSentences: Elias, a lonely, unkempt techie, lived in a secluded cabin during the harsh winter, subsisting on blueberry muffins and stale donuts with strong arabica coffee. His days were spent staring at an old browser on his screen, a calculator nearby, obsessing over the monetary value of an ancient artifact he’d found. He often drifted into a pleasant fantasy of a better life, a stark contrast to his current unhappiness. A faded photograph of an obelisk on his wall served as a cryptic reminder of an old issue. He used to be a muscle-bound security expert, a life he left behind, now just lifting weights made from scrap metal covered in wool. He felt naturally drawn to investigate the indirect clues he'd gathered about the artifact's true origin, a way to broaden his purpose.

The tranquility was shattered when news broke: the notorious kingpin, a cutthroat mastermind known for betrayal, had resurfaced, seeking vengeance. The criminal underworld was abuzz. Elias knew this meant his past was catching up. The unsettling noise of high-powered engines outside, at the junction near the old gate by the railroad tracks, signaled trouble. He saw a frigate of black SUVs, their engines revving with petrol and ignition engaged, moving forwards. It was the cardinal sign of an attack, led by an iconic enforcer. Elias felt his heart utterly twisted as he realized his old limiter of self-imposed isolation was now kaput. He had been kicked back into the game, hearing their distant heckle and mocking laughter.

Elias, driven by a deep sense of loyalty to an old partner and the need to rescue something precious, made a stand. He fortified his safe house, a former hangar near an old embassy, now adorned with a single gladiolus in a miniature vase. The attackers were jackhammering at the reinforced brick walls. Elias, with the magic of his improvised tech, turned the tide. He launched explosive coconut shells and strategically placed firestone charges, creating an enormous diversion. He needed to outlast them, fueled by memories of shared honour. He knew the keystone to their plans was an ancient map he possessed, which he protected like an apple. He saw their models falter, their resolve beginning to bloom.

As the dust settled, Elias surveyed the darkened, debris-strewn hangar. He had won, but not inexpensively. The iconic kingpin was gone, leaving only vermin behind. His victory felt hopeful, and the silence, once a burden, now held a new philosophy. He brewed some cookery, a simple bacon and pickle dish, contemplating his next move. He was no longer just a viewer of his own life; he was its gamekeeper. The novelty of retirement had worn off. He felt a quiet satisfaction, knowing he had protected something round and blunt – the truth. He swore to hunt down the remaining operatives, inching closer to a final peace. His life, utterly changed, was now featuring a new path. He’d been ticked off the list of the forgotten, no longer a mere jongleur in the shadows.
rawPanelSentences: Elias, a **lonely**, **unkempt** **techie**, lived in a secluded cabin during the harsh **winter**, subsisting on **blueberry** muffins and stale **donuts** with strong **arabica** coffee. His days were spent staring at an old **browser** on his screen, a **calculator** nearby, obsessing over the **monetary** value of an **ancient** **artifact** he’d found. He often drifted into a pleasant **fantasy** of a better life, a stark contrast to his current **unhappiness**. A faded **photograph** of an **obelisk** on his wall served as a cryptic reminder of an old **issue**. He used to be a **muscle**-bound security expert, a life he left behind, now just lifting **weights** made from scrap metal covered in **wool**. He felt **naturally** drawn to **investigate** the **indirect** clues he'd gathered about the artifact's true origin, a way to **broaden** his purpose.

The tranquility was shattered when news broke: the **notorious** **kingpin**, a **cutthroat** **mastermind** known for **betrayal**, had resurfaced, seeking **vengeance**. The criminal underworld was **abuzz**. Elias knew this meant his past was catching up. The unsettling **noise** of high-powered engines outside, at the **junction** near the old **gate** by the **railroad** tracks, signaled trouble. He saw a **frigate** of black SUVs, their engines revving with **petrol** and **ignition** engaged, moving **forwards**. It was the **cardinal** sign of an attack, led by an **iconic** enforcer. Elias felt his heart **utterly** **twisted** as he realized his old **limiter** of self-imposed isolation was now **kaput**. He had been **kicked** back into the game, hearing their distant **heckle** and **mocking** laughter.

Elias, driven by a deep sense of **loyalty** to an old partner and the need to **rescue** something precious, made a stand. He fortified his **safe** house, a former **hangar** near an old **embassy**, now adorned with a single **gladiolus** in a **miniature** vase. The attackers were **jackhammering** at the reinforced **brick** walls. Elias, with the **magic** of his improvised tech, turned the tide. He launched explosive **coconut** shells and strategically placed **firestone** charges, creating an **enormous** diversion. He needed to **outlast** them, fueled by memories of shared **honour**. He knew the **keystone** to their plans was an **ancient** map he possessed, which he protected like an **apple**. He saw their **models** falter, their resolve beginning to **bloom**.

As the dust settled, Elias surveyed the **darkened**, debris-strewn **hangar**. He had won, but not **inexpensively**. The **iconic** **kingpin** was gone, leaving only **vermin** behind. His victory felt **hopeful**, and the silence, once a burden, now held a new **philosophy**. He brewed some **cookery**, a simple **bacon** and **pickle** dish, contemplating his next move. He was no longer just a **viewer** of his own life; he was its **gamekeeper**. The **novelty** of retirement had worn off. He felt a quiet satisfaction, knowing he had protected something **round** and **blunt** – the truth. He swore to **hunt** down the remaining operatives, **inching** closer to a final peace. His life, **utterly** changed, was now **featuring** a new path. He’d been **ticked** off the list of the forgotten, no longer a mere **jongleur** in the shadows.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A middle-aged man with a grizzled beard, weary eyes, and a worn leather jacket. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elias, a middle-aged man with a grizzled beard, wearing a worn leather jacket, sits alone in a cluttered, dimly lit cabin, gazing intensely at an ancient artifact on a desk, a dusty laptop screen reflecting his weary face. (medium shot, slightly high angle). TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, grim-faced, peers through a frosted window at a convoy of black SUVs pulling up to a remote railroad crossing at dusk, the vehicle headlights cutting through the snow-laden air. (over-the-shoulder shot from inside the cabin, looking out). BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias, sweat beading on his forehead, activates a homemade explosive device from behind a reinforced brick wall inside his repurposed hangar, explosions and debris filling the background where shadowy adult figures are attacking. (dynamic low-angle shot, close-up on Elias's determined face and hands). BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, a few days older, with a thoughtful expression and deep lines etched around his eyes, sits at a simple metal table in the now quiet, still-damaged hangar, eating a plate of bacon and eggs, a single gladiolus in a small vase nearby. (eye-level medium shot).

definitions.definition: An edible round berry, belonging to the cowberry group (Vaccinium sect. Cyanococcus), with flared crowns at the end, that turns blue on ripening.
The shrub of the above-mentioned berry.
A dark blue colour.
To gather or forage for blueberries.
Of a dark blue colour.
An act or episode of rescuing, saving.
A liberation, freeing.
The forcible ending of a siege; liberation from similar military peril
definitions.example: The rescue of Jerusalem was the original motive of the Crusaders

definitions.definition: A special airliner flight to bring home passengers who are stranded
A rescuee.
definitions.example: The dog was a rescue with some behavior issues.

definitions.definition: To save from any violence, danger or evil.
definitions.example: The well-trained team rescued everyone after the avalanche.

definitions.definition: To free or liberate from confinement or other physical restraint.
definitions.example: to rescue a prisoner from the enemy.

definitions.definition: To recover forcibly.
To deliver by arms, notably from a siege.
To remove or withdraw from a state of exposure to evil and sin.
definitions.example: Traditionally missionaries aim to rescue many ignorant heathen souls.

definitions.definition: To achieve something positive under difficult conditions.
The state of being loyal; fidelity.
Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
That which limits or confines.
A circuit that allows signals below a specified input threshold to pass unaffected while attenuating the peaks of stronger signals.
A friar who had a license to beg within certain bounds.
A box, usually made of metal, in which valuables can be locked for safekeeping.
A condom.
A ventilated or refrigerated chest or closet for securing provisions from noxious animals or insects.
A safety bicycle.
To make something safe.
Not in danger; out of harm's reach.
definitions.example: You’ll be safe here.

definitions.definition: Free from risk.
definitions.example: It’s safe to eat this.

definitions.definition: Providing protection from danger; providing shelter.
definitions.example: We have to find a safe spot, where we can hide out until this is over.

definitions.definition: When a batter successfully reaches first base, or when a baserunner successfully advances to the next base or returns to the base he last occupied; not out.
definitions.example: The pitcher attempted to pick off the runner at first, but he was safe.

definitions.definition: Properly secured.
definitions.example: The documents are safe.

definitions.definition: (used after a noun, often forming a compound) Not susceptible to a specified source of harm.
definitions.example: dishwasher-safe

definitions.definition: Great, cool, awesome, respectable; a term of approbation, often as interjection.
Lenient, usually describing a teacher that is easy-going.
Reliable; trusty.
Cautious.
Of a programming language, type-safe or more generally offering well-defined behavior despite programming errors.
Recognition of importance or value; respect; veneration (of someone, usually for being morally upright or successful)
definitions.example: The crowds gave the returning general much honor and praise.

definitions.definition: The state of being morally upright, honest, noble, virtuous, and magnanimous; excellence of character; the perception of such a state; favourable reputation; dignity
definitions.example: He was a most perfect knight, for he had great honor and chivalry.

definitions.definition: A token of praise or respect; something that represents praiseworthiness or respect, such as a prize or award given by the state to a citizen
definitions.example: Audie Murphy received many honors, such as the Distinguished Service Cross.

definitions.definition: A privilege
definitions.example: I had the honour of dining with the ambassador.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) the privilege of going first
definitions.example: I'll let you have the honours, Bob—go ahead.

definitions.definition: A cause of respect and fame; a glory; an excellency; an ornament.
definitions.example: He is an honour to his nation.

definitions.definition: (feudal law) a seigniory or lordship held of the king, on which other lordships and manors depended
The center point of the upper half of an armorial escutcheon (compare honour point)
In bridge, an ace, king, queen, jack, or ten especially of the trump suit. In some other games, an ace, king, queen or jack.
(in the plural) (courses for) an honours degree: a university qualification of the highest rank
definitions.example: At university I took honours in modern history.

definitions.definition: To think of highly, to respect highly; to show respect for; to recognise the importance or spiritual value of
definitions.example: The freedom fighters will be forever remembered and honored by the people.

definitions.definition: To conform to, abide by, act in accordance with (an agreement, treaty, promise, request, or the like)
definitions.example: I trusted you, but you have not honored your promise.

definitions.definition: To confer (bestow) an honour or privilege upon (someone)
definitions.example: Ten members of the profession were honored at the ceremony.

definitions.definition: To make payment in respect of (a cheque, banker's draft, etc.)
definitions.example: I'm sorry Sir, but the bank did not honour your cheque.

definitions.definition: Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.
An appliance to be fixed on the front of a grate, to keep a kettle warm, etc.
To spend the winter (in a particular place).
definitions.example: When they retired, they hoped to winter in Florida.

definitions.definition: To store something (for instance animals) somewhere over winter to protect it from cold.
In an inexpensive manner.
Relating to, or having the characteristics of, an icon.
Famously and distinctively representative of its type.
Representing something; symbolic.
definitions.example: an iconic gesture in sign language

definitions.definition: A murderer who slits the throats of victims.
An unscrupulous, ruthless or unethical person.
A three-player pocket billiards game where the object is to be the last player with at least one ball still on the table.
Involving the cutting of throats
Of or relating to a card game where everyone plays for him or herself rather than playing with a partner.
definitions.example: He found that playing cutthroat Spades was much more difficult than playing with a partner.

definitions.definition: Ruthlessly competitive, dog-eat-dog
definitions.example: Law is a cutthroat business, you always have to look out to see who is trying to outdo you.

definitions.definition: A mechanical or electronic device that performs mathematical calculations.
A person who performs mathematical calculation
A person who calculates (in the sense of scheming).
A set of mathematical tables.
Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
definitions.example: He knew that it was trash day, when the garbage collectors made all the noise.

definitions.definition: Sound or signal generated by random fluctuations.
Unwanted part of a signal.
(by extension) Unwanted fuss or bustle; useless activity.
The measured level of variation in gene expression among cells, regardless of source, within a supposedly identical population.
Rumour or complaint.
definitions.example: The problems with the new computer system are causing a lot of noise at Head Office.

definitions.definition: Music, in general; a concert; also, a company of musicians; a band.
A genre of rock music that uses static and other non-musical sounds, also influenced by art rock.
To make a noise; to sound.
To spread news of; to spread as rumor or gossip.
An itinerant entertainer in medieval England and France; roles included song, music, acrobatics etc.; a troubadour.
A juggler; a conjurer.
A mountebank.
The act of joining, or the state of being joined.
A place where two things meet, especially where two roads meet.
The boundary between two physically different materials, especially between conductors, semiconductors, or metals.
The place where a distributary departs from the main stream.
A place where two or more railways or railroads meet.
A point in time between two unrelated consecutive broadcasts.
A kind of symbolic link to a directory.
In the Raku programming language, a construct representing a composite of several values connected by an operator.
(of roads or tracks) To form a junction.
Together, acting as one; collectively.
definitions.example: They jointly raised the child, even though they were no longer married.

definitions.definition: A doorlike structure outside a house.
Doorway, opening, or passage in a fence or wall.
Movable barrier.
definitions.example: The gate in front of the railroad crossing went up after the train had passed.

definitions.definition: A logical pathway made up of switches which turn on or off. Examples are and, or, nand, etc.
The gap between a batsman's bat and pad.
definitions.example: Singh was bowled through the gate, a very disappointing way for a world-class batsman to get out.

definitions.definition: The amount of money made by selling tickets to a concert or a sports event.
(flow cytometry) A line that separates particle type-clusters on two-dimensional dot plots.
Passageway (as in an air terminal) where passengers can embark or disembark.
The controlling terminal of a field effect transistor (FET).
In a lock tumbler, the opening for the stump of the bolt to pass through or into.
The channel or opening through which metal is poured into the mould; the ingate.
The waste piece of metal cast in the opening; a sprue or sullage piece. Also written geat and git.
A mechanism, in a film camera and projector, that holds each frame momentarily stationary behind the aperture.
A tally mark consisting of four vertical bars crossed by a diagonal, representing a count of five.
To keep something inside by means of a closed gate.
To punish, especially a child or teenager, by not allowing them to go out.
To open a closed ion channel.
To furnish with a gate.
To turn (an image intensifier) on and off selectively as needed, or to avoid damage. See autogating.
The center part of the sternum.
Any of several flowering plants, of the genus Gladiolus, having sword-shaped leaves and showy flowers on spikes; gladiola.
Someone who views a spectacle; an onlooker or spectator.
Someone who watches television.
Any optical device used to view photographic slides.
A program that displays the contents of a file.
The manager of a colliery, who directs its workings and ventilation.
Deviating from the norm; unusual, extraordinary.
Exceedingly wicked; atrocious or outrageous.
Extremely large; greatly exceeding the common size, extent, etc.
A person or animal who browses.
A person who examines goods for sale but purchases nothing.
A web browser.
A fencer's practice foil with a soft tip.
A short needle with a strong point.
(smoking) A marijuana cigar.
definitions.example: 2005: to make his point, lead rapper B-Real fired up a blunt in front of the cameras and several hundred thousand people and announced, “I'm taking a hit for every one of y'all!” — Martin Torgoff, Can't Find My Way Home (Simon & Schuster 2005, p. 461)

definitions.definition: Money
A playboating move resembling a cartwheel performed on a wave.
Having a thick edge or point; not sharp.
Dull in understanding; slow of discernment; opposed to acute.
Abrupt in address; plain; unceremonious; wanting the forms of civility; rough in manners or speech.
definitions.example: the blunt admission that he had never liked my company

definitions.definition: Hard to impress or penetrate.
Slow or deficient in feeling: insensitive.
A genre of music originating from the South (United States) and mixing elements of rock, blues, country, hillbilly boogie and bluegrass music.
A member of the subculture associated with rockabilly music.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.
definitions.example: Many railroads roughly follow the trace of older land - and/or water roads

definitions.definition: The transportation system comprising such roads and vehicles fitted to travel on the rails, usually with several vehicles connected together in a train.
A single, privately or publicly owned property comprising one or more such roads and usually associated assets
definitions.example: Railroads can only compete fully if their tracks are technically compatible with and linked to each-other

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.
definitions.example: The lawyers made the procedure a railroad to get the signatures they needed.

definitions.definition: To transport via railroad.
To operate a railroad.
definitions.example: The Thatcherite experiment proved the private sector can railroad as inefficiently as a state monopoly

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.
To travel by railroad.
To engage in a hobby pertaining to railroads.
To manipulate and hasten a procedure, as of formal approval of a law or resolution.
definitions.example: The majority railroaded the bill through parliament, without the customary expert studies which would delay it till after the elections.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.
definitions.example: They could only convict him by railroading him on suspect drug-possession charges.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.
definitions.example: He was railroaded into signing a non-disclosure agreement at his exit interview.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.
(upholstery) To run fabric horizontally instead of the usual vertically.
The application of rituals or actions, especially those based on occult knowledge, to subdue or manipulate natural or supernatural beings and forces in order to have some benefit from them
A specific ritual or procedure associated with such magic; a spell.
The supernatural forces which are drawn on in such a ritual
Something producing successful and remarkable results, especially when not fully understood; an enchanting quality; exceptional skill.
A conjuring trick or illusion performed to give the appearance of supernatural phenomena or powers.
The art or practice of performing conjuring tricks and illusions.
Complicated or esoteric code that is not expected to be generally understood.
To produce, transform (something), (as if) by magic.
Having supernatural talents, properties or qualities attributed to magic.
Producing extraordinary results, as though through the use of magic; wonderful, amazing.
definitions.example: a magic moment

definitions.definition: Pertaining to conjuring tricks or illusions performed for entertainment etc.
definitions.example: a magic show; a magic trick

definitions.definition: Great; excellent.
definitions.example: — I cleaned up the flat while you were out. — Really? Magic!

definitions.definition: Describing the number of nucleons in a particularly stable isotopic nucleus; 2, 8, 20, 28, 50, 82, 126, and 184.
Being a literal number or string value with no meaning or context, not defined as a constant or variable
definitions.example: The code is full of magic numbers and we can't figure out what they mean.

definitions.definition: A species of coffee plant, Coffea arabica, native to Ethiopia
The bean of this plant
Coffee made from these coffee beans
A large garage-like structure where aircraft are kept.
definitions.example: The plane taxied on over to the hangar for repairs.

definitions.definition: A covered shed for carriages.
To store (an aircraft) in a hangar.
Out of order; not working.
definitions.example: Her marriage went kaput.

definitions.definition: A tree-dwelling marsupial, Phascolarctos cinereus, that resembles a small bear with a broad head, large ears and sharp claws, mainly found in eastern Australia.

One of the officials appointed by the pope in the Roman Catholic Church, ranking only below the pope and the patriarchs, constituting the special college which elects the pope. (See Wikipedia article on Catholic cardinals.)
Any of a genus of songbirds of the finch family, Cardinalis.
Any of various related passerine birds of the family Cardinalidae (See Wikipedia article on cardinals) and other similar birds that were once considered to be related.
(color) A deep red color, somewhat less vivid than scarlet, the traditional colour of a Catholic cardinal's cassock. (same as cardinal red)
Short for cardinal number, a number indicating quantity, or the size of a set (e.g., zero, one, two, three). (See Wikipedia article on Cardinal number.)
(grammar) Short for cardinal numeral, a word used to represent a cardinal number.
Short for cardinal flower (Lobelia cardinalis), a flowering plant.
Short for cardinal tetra (Paracheirodon axelrodi), a freshwater fish.
(bow) A woman's short cloak with a hood, originally made of scarlet cloth.
Mulled red wine.
Of fundamental importance; crucial, pivotal.
definitions.example: a cardinal rule

definitions.definition: Of or relating to the cardinal directions (north, south, east and west).
definitions.example: a cardinal mark

definitions.definition: Describing a "natural" number used to indicate quantity (e.g., zero, one, two, three), as opposed to an ordinal number indicating relative position.
Having a bright red color (from the color of a Catholic cardinal's cassock).
The art and practice of preparing food for consumption, especially by the application of heat; cooking.
definitions.example: Henry was not very good at cookery and most of his meals ended up burned.

definitions.definition: A delicacy; a dainty.
Cooking tools or apparatus.
Revenge taken for an insult, injury, or other wrong.
Desire for revenge.
Toward the front.
In a progressive direction.
An obsolete type of sailing warship with a single continuous gun deck, typically used for patrolling, blockading, etc, but not in line of battle.

A 19th-century warship combining sail and steam propulsion, typically of ironclad timber construction, supplementing and superseding sailing ships of the battle line until made obsolete by the development of the solely steam-propelled iron battleship.
A modern type of warship, smaller than a destroyer, originally (WWII) introduced as an anti-submarine vessel but now general purpose.
A frigatebird.
So as to form a circle or trace a circular path, or approximation thereof.
definitions.example: High above, vultures circled around.

definitions.definition: So as to surround or be near.
definitions.example: Everybody please gather around.

definitions.definition: Nearly; approximately; about.
definitions.example: An adult elephant weighs around five tons.

definitions.definition: From place to place.
definitions.example: Look around and see what you find.

definitions.definition: From one state or condition to an opposite or very different one; with a metaphorical change in direction; bringing about awareness or agreement.
definitions.example: He used to stay up late but his new girlfriend changed that around.

definitions.definition: (with turn, spin etc.) So as to partially or completely rotate; so as to face in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: She spun around a few times.

definitions.definition: Used with verbs to indicate repeated or continuous action, or in numerous locations or with numerous people.
definitions.example: I asked around, and no-one really liked it.

definitions.definition: Used with certain verbs to suggest unproductive activity.
Defining a circle or closed curve containing a thing.
definitions.example: I planted a row of lilies around the statue.

definitions.definition: (of abstract things) Centred upon; surrounding.
definitions.example: There has been a lot of controversy around the handling of personal information.

definitions.definition: Following the perimeter of a specified area and returning to the starting point.
definitions.example: She went around the track fifty times.

definitions.definition: Following a path which curves near an object, with the object on the inside of the curve.
definitions.example: The road took a brief detour around the large rock formation, then went straight on.

definitions.definition: Near; in the vicinity of.
definitions.example: I don't want you around me.

definitions.definition: At or to various places within.
definitions.example: She went around the office and got everyone to sign the card.

definitions.definition: A circular or spherical object or part of an object.
A circular or repetitious route.
definitions.example: The guards have started their rounds; the prisoner should be caught soon.

definitions.definition: A general outburst from a group of people at an event.
definitions.example: The candidate got a round of applause after every sentence or two.

definitions.definition: A song that is sung by groups of people with each subset of people starting at a different time.
A serving of something; a portion of something to each person in a group.
definitions.example: They brought us a round of drinks about every thirty minutes.

definitions.definition: A single individual portion or dose of medicine.
One sandwich (two full slices of bread with filling).
A long-bristled, circular-headed paintbrush used in oil and acrylic painting.
A firearm cartridge, bullet, or any individual ammunition projectile. Originally referring to the spherical projectile ball of a smoothbore firearm. Compare round shot and solid shot.
One of the specified pre-determined segments of the total time of a sport event, such as a boxing or wrestling match, during which contestants compete before being signaled to stop.
A stage, level, set of events in a game
(drafting, CAD) A rounded relief or cut at an edge, especially an outside edge, added for a finished appearance and to soften sharp edges.
A strip of material with a circular face that covers an edge, gap, or crevice for decorative, sanitary, or security purposes.

Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.
definitions.example: He played a slow but sure knock of 35.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.
definitions.example: Don’t knock it until you’ve tried it.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.
To bump or impact.
definitions.example: I accidentally knocked my drink off the bar.

definitions.definition: To rap one's knuckles against something, especially wood.
definitions.example: Knock on the door and find out if they’re home.

definitions.definition: The top stone of an arch.
Something on which other things depend for support.
A native or resident of the American state of Pennsylvania.
(retail) A retail price that is double the cost price; a markup of 100%.
The combination of the shortstop and second baseman.
To distort (an image) by projecting it onto a surface at an angle, which for example causes a square to look like a trapezoid.
(retail) To double the cost price in order to determine the retail price; to apply a markup of 100%.
The action or an instance of flowing or coming out, an outflow, particularly:
Someone or something that flows out or comes out, particularly:
The means or opportunity by which something flows or comes out, particularly:
The place where something flows or comes out, an outlet, particularly:
The action or an instance of sending something out, particularly:
definitions.example: The issue of the directive from the treasury prompted the central bank's most recent issue of currency.

definitions.definition: Any question or situation to be resolved, particularly:
definitions.example: Please stand by. We are having technical issues.

definitions.definition: The action or an instance of concluding something, particularly:
The end result of an event or events, any result or outcome, particularly:
The action or an instance of feeling some emotion.
The action or an instance of leaving any state or condition.
To flow out, to proceed from, to come out or from.
definitions.example: The rents issuing from the land permitted him to live as a man of independent means.

definitions.definition: To rush out, to sally forth.
definitions.example: The men issued from the town and attacked the besiegers.

definitions.definition: To extend into, to open onto.
definitions.example: The road issues into the highway.

definitions.definition: To turn out in a certain way, to result in.
To come to a point in fact or law on which the parties join issue.
To send out; to put into circulation.
To deliver for use.
definitions.example: The prison issued new uniforms for the inmates.

definitions.definition: To deliver by authority.
definitions.example: The court issued a writ of mandamus.

definitions.definition: Petroleum, a fluid consisting of a mixture of refined petroleum hydrocarbons, primarily consisting of octane, commonly used as a motor fuel.
A motor vehicle powered by petrol (as opposed to diesel).
In a natural manner.
definitions.example: Although he was unused to the situation, he tried to act naturally.

definitions.definition: Inherently or by nature.
definitions.example: Boys are naturally aggressive.

definitions.definition: Surely or without any doubt.
definitions.example: I shall naturally protest at that decision.

definitions.definition: The state of being new or novel; newness.
A new product; an innovation.
A small mass-produced trinket.
In novelty theory, newness, density of complexification, and dynamic change as opposed to static habituation.
Any one of various common types of small insects or animals which cause harm and annoyance.
definitions.example: The area was plagued by all sorts of vermin: fleas, lice, mice, and rats to name a few.

definitions.definition: Animals that prey on game, such as foxes or weasels.
Obnoxious, or mean and offensive person or people.
definitions.example: Bring these vermin to the Palace of Justice.
